response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software engineer with strong web/backend craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, distributed systems) and genuine high-performance, multi-threaded, low-latency work via DLang. However, the role centers on modern C++/STL development with industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443, SDL), message brokers, and MQTT/IoT protocols — none of which the resume demonstrates. C++ exposure is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago and a university thesis. The core tech stack and security/industrial-automation requirements are largely absent, making this a poor fit despite a solid general engineering background.
strengths: Deep, broad software engineering experience with strong testing and CI/CD craftsmanship
Proven high-performance, multi-threaded, low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching)
Distributed/event-driven and microservices background handling large-scale traffic
concerns: Minimal and outdated C++/STL experience; primary stack is JS/TS and DLang
No demonstrated message brokers, MQTT/IoT, or industrial automation protocol experience
No secure-development-lifecycle or IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity background
